name: Interval
summary: Represents an interval in one-dimensional space, that is defined as two extrema or bounds.

constructors.name: Interval(Interval)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance copying the other instance values.

params.name: other
params.doc: The Rhino.Geometry.Interval to use as a base.

constructors.name: Interval(Double, Double)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Rhino.Geometry.Interval class.

methods.name: CompareTo
methods.signature: public int CompareTo(Interval other)
methods.summary: Compares this Interval with another interval. The lower bound has first evaluation priority.

params.name: other
params.doc: The other Interval to compare with.

returns.doc: 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this[0] < other[0]+1: if this[0] > other[0]-1: if this[0] == other[0] and this[1] < other[1]+1: if this[0] == other[0] and this[1] > other[1].

methods.name: EpsilonEquals
methods.signature: public bool EpsilonEquals(Interval other, double epsilon)
methods.summary: Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Interval.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Interval,System.Double)"]

methods.name: Equals(Object)
methods.signature: public override bool Equals(Object obj)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified Object is equal to the current Interval, comparing by value.

params.name: obj
params.doc: The other object to compare with.

returns.doc: true if obj is an Interval and has the same bounds; false otherwise.

methods.name: Equals(Interval)
methods.signature: public bool Equals(Interval other)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified Interval is equal to the current Interval, comparing by value.

params.name: other
params.doc: The other interval to compare with.

returns.doc: true if obj is an Interval and has the same bounds; false otherwise.

methods.name: FromIntersection
methods.signature: public static Interval FromIntersection(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Returns a new Interval that is the Intersection of the two input Intervals.

returns.doc: If the intersection is not empty, then intersection = [max(a.Min(),b.Min()), min(a.Max(),b.Max())] The interval [ON.UnsetValue,ON.UnsetValue] is considered to be the empty set interval. The result of any intersection involving an empty set interval or disjoint intervals is the empty set interval.

methods.name: FromUnion
methods.signature: public static Interval FromUnion(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Returns a new Interval which contains both inputs.

returns.doc: The union of an empty set and an increasing interval is the increasing interval. The union of two empty sets is empty.The union of an empty set an a non-empty interval is the non-empty interval.The union of two non-empty intervals is [min(a.Min(),b.Min()), max(a.Max(),b.Max())]

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.signature: public override int GetHashCode()
methods.summary: Computes the hash code for this Interval object.

returns.doc: A hash value that might be equal for two different Interval values.

methods.name: Grow
methods.signature: public void Grow(double value)
methods.summary: Grows the Interval to include the given number.

params.name: value
params.doc: Number to include in this interval.

methods.name: IncludesInterval(Interval)
methods.signature: public bool IncludesInterval(Interval interval)
methods.summary: Tests another interval for Interval inclusion.

returns.doc: true if the other interval is contained within or is coincident with the limits of this Interval; otherwise false.

methods.name: IncludesInterval(Interval, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool IncludesInterval(Interval interval, bool strict)
methods.summary: Tests another interval for Interval inclusion.

params.name: strict
params.doc: If true, the other interval must be fully on the inside of the Interval.

returns.doc: true if the other interval is contained within the limits of this Interval; otherwise false.

methods.name: IncludesParameter(Double)
methods.signature: public bool IncludesParameter(double t)
methods.summary: Tests a parameter for Interval inclusion.

returns.doc: true if t is contained within or is coincident with the limits of this Interval.

methods.name: IncludesParameter(Double, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool IncludesParameter(double t, bool strict)
methods.summary: Tests a parameter for Interval inclusion.

params.name: strict
params.doc: If true, the parameter must be fully on the inside of the Interval.

returns.doc: true if t is contained within the limits of this Interval.

methods.name: MakeIncreasing
methods.signature: public void MakeIncreasing()
methods.summary: Ensures this Interval is either singleton or increasing.

methods.name: NormalizedIntervalAt
methods.signature: public Interval NormalizedIntervalAt(Interval intervalParameter)
methods.summary: Converts interval value, or pair of values, to normalized parameter.

returns.doc: Normalized parameter x so that min*(1.0-x) + max*x = intervalParameter.

methods.name: NormalizedParameterAt
methods.signature: public double NormalizedParameterAt(double intervalParameter)
methods.summary: Converts interval value, or pair of values, to normalized parameter.

returns.doc: Normalized parameter x so that min*(1.0-x) + max*x = intervalParameter.

methods.name: ParameterAt
methods.signature: public double ParameterAt(double normalizedParameter)
methods.summary: Converts normalized parameter to interval value, or pair of values.

returns.doc: Interval parameter min*(1.0-normalizedParameter) + max*normalizedParameter.

methods.name: ParameterIntervalAt
methods.signature: public Interval ParameterIntervalAt(Interval normalizedInterval)
methods.summary: Converts normalized parameter to interval value, or pair of values.

returns.doc: Interval parameter min*(1.0-normalizedParameter) + max*normalized_paramete.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.signature: public void Reverse()
methods.summary: Changes interval to [-T1, -T0].

methods.name: Swap
methods.signature: public void Swap()
methods.summary: Exchanges T0 and T1.

methods.name: ToString
methods.signature: public override string ToString()
methods.summary: Returns a string representation of this Interval.

returns.doc: A string with T0,T1.

methods.name: Addition(Double, Interval)
methods.signature: public static Interval operator +(double number, Interval interval)
methods.summary: Shifts an interval by a specific amount (addition).

params.name: interval
params.doc: The interval to be used as a base.

returns.doc: A new interval where T0 and T1 are summed with number.

methods.name: Addition(Interval, Double)
methods.signature: public static Interval operator +(Interval interval, double number)
methods.summary: Shifts a Interval by a specific amount (addition).

params.name: interval
params.doc: The interval to be used as a base.

returns.doc: A new interval where T0 and T1 are summed with number.

methods.name: Equality
methods.signature: public static bool operator ==(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the two Intervals have equal values.

returns.doc: true if the components of the two intervals are exactly equal; otherwise false.

methods.name: GreaterThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator >(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified Interval comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second Interval. The lower bound has first evaluation priority.

returns.doc: true if a[0] is larger than b[0], or a[0] == b[0] and a[1] is larger than b[1]; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GreaterThanOrEqual
methods.signature: public static bool operator >=(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified Interval comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second Interval, or is equal to it. The lower bound has first evaluation priority.

returns.doc: true if a[0] is larger than b[0], or a[0] == b[0] and a[1] is larger than or equal to b[1]; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Inequality
methods.signature: public static bool operator !=(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the two Intervals have different values.

returns.doc: true if the two intervals are different in any value; false if they are equal.

methods.name: LessThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator <(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified Interval comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second Interval. The lower bound has first evaluation priority.

returns.doc: true if a[0] is smaller than b[0], or a[0] == b[0] and a[1] is smaller than b[1]; otherwise, false.

methods.name: LessThanOrEqual
methods.signature: public static bool operator <=(Interval a, Interval b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified Interval comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second Interval, or is equal to it. The lower bound has first evaluation priority.

returns.doc: true if a[0] is smaller than b[0], or a[0] == b[0] and a[1] is smaller than or equal to b[1]; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Subtraction(Double, Interval)
methods.signature: public static Interval operator -(double number, Interval interval)
methods.summary: Shifts an interval by a specific amount (subtraction).

params.name: number
params.doc: The shifting value to subtract from (minuend).

params.name: interval
params.doc: The interval to be subtracted from (subtrahend).

returns.doc: A new interval with [number-T0, number-T1].

methods.name: Subtraction(Interval, Double)
methods.signature: public static Interval operator -(Interval interval, double number)
methods.summary: Shifts an interval by a specific amount (subtraction).

params.name: number
params.doc: The shifting value to be subtracted (subtrahend).

returns.doc: A new interval with [T0-number, T1-number].

properties.name: IsSingleton
properties.summary: Returns true if T0 == T1 != ON.UnsetValue.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this Interval is valid. Valid intervals must contain valid numbers.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or sets the indexed bound of this Interval.

properties.name: Length
properties.summary: Gets the signed length of the numeric range. If the interval is decreasing, a negative length will be returned.

properties.name: Max
properties.summary: Gets the larger of T0 and T1.

properties.name: Mid
properties.summary: Gets the average of T0 and T1.

properties.name: Min
properties.summary: Gets the smaller of T0 and T1.

properties.name: T0
properties.summary: Gets or sets the lower bound of the Interval.

properties.name: T1
properties.summary: Gets or sets the upper bound of the Interval.

properties.name: Unset
properties.summary: Gets an Interval whose limits are RhinoMath.UnsetValue.
